docs.name: expand
docs.full: Generates all unique combinations of the provided columns or expressions. Supports nesting() to only include combinations present in the data.

params.description: | Vector | Call Specification of columns to expand.

docs.name: ntile
docs.full: Divides the vector into n buckets.

docs.name: greet
docs.full: A placeholder function that returns a friendly greeting.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the person to greet.

docs.name: read_file
docs.full: Reads the entire content of a file into a string.

docs.name: atan2
docs.full: Compute `atan2(y, x)` with quadrant-aware angle.

return.description: | Vector | NDArray Computed result (scalar or vectorized).

docs.name: fct_reorder
docs.brief: Order factor levels by another vector
docs.full: Reorders factor levels using summary statistics computed from a companion numeric vector.

docs.name: str_extract
docs.full: Returns the first regular-expression match found in each string.

docs.name: rm
docs.full: Removes one or more variables from the current environment by name. Supports bare symbols (R-style selective removal), strings, and lists of names via the `list` parameter.

params.description: | String One or more variables to remove.

params.name: list
params.description: (Optional) A list of variable names to remove.

examples: rm(x, y)          -- Removes x and y

docs.name: separate_rows
docs.full: Expands delimited string values into multiple rows while repeating the remaining columns.

docs.name: shape
docs.full: Returns the shape of an NDArray as a list of integers.

docs.name: deserialize_from_file
docs.full: Reads a serialized T value from a file.

docs.name: patch
docs.full: Replaces matching nodes in one pipeline with definitions from another pipeline.

docs.name: floor_date
docs.full: Rounds Date or Datetime values down to the requested unit boundary.

docs.name: wald_test
docs.full: Tests a null hypothesis that a subset of coefficients are jointly equal to zero.

params.name: value
params.description: (Optional) The null value to test against. Defaults to 0.0.

return.description: A one-row DataFrame with the test statistic and p-value.

docs.name: replace_na
docs.full: replace_na() replaces missing values with specified values.

params.name: replace
params.description: A list of named values to use for replacing NA.

docs.name: node
docs.full: Configure execution settings such as the runtime and custom serialized methods for a pipeline node. This function is typically used directly within a `pipeline { ... }` block to wrap expressions, enable cross-runtime evaluation, and optionally render a `.qmd` document via `runtime = Quarto`.

params.name: command
params.description: (Optional) The expression to evaluate inside the node. Mutually exclusive with `script`.

params.name: script
params.description: (Optional) Path to an external `.R`, `.py`, or `.qmd` file to execute as the node body. Mutually exclusive with `command`. The runtime is auto-detected from the file extension when not explicitly provided.

params.name: runtime
params.description: (Optional) The runtime environment (T, R, Python, Quarto). Default = T.

params.name: serializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom serializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: deserializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom deserializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: args
params.description: (Optional) Runtime/tool arguments. For Quarto, use this to pass CLI arguments such as `subcommand`, `path`, and additional options. `output_dir` is reserved and managed automatically so the rendered result is stored as the node artifact.

params.name: functions
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Files to source before execution.

params.name: include
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Additional files for the sandbox.

params.name: noop
params.description: (Optional) Whether to skip execution and generate a stub. Default = false.

return.description: The evaluated return value of the command.

docs.name: nesting
docs.brief: Helper to find combinations present in data
docs.full: nesting() is used inside expand() or complete() to only use combinations that already appear in the data.

docs.name: colnames
docs.full: Returns a list of column names in the DataFrame.

docs.name: col_lens
docs.full: Targets a column in a DataFrame or a key in a Dictionary.

return.description: A lens for the specified column/key.

docs.name: fct
docs.full: Creates a factor whose levels follow the first appearance order of the input values.

docs.name: select_node
docs.full: Returns a DataFrame summarising the requested metadata fields for all nodes in the pipeline. This is a read-only inspection operation — it does not return a Pipeline.  Available fields: `$name`, `$runtime`, `$serializer`, `$deserializer`, `$noop`, `$deps`, `$depth`, `$command_type`.

params.description: One or more metadata field references (e.g. `$name`, `$runtime`).

return.description: A DataFrame with the requested metadata columns.

docs.name: na_float
docs.full: Represents a missing float value.

docs.name: bind_rows
docs.full: Appends rows from multiple DataFrames into a single DataFrame.

docs.name: pipeline_print
docs.full: Prints a human-readable summary of the pipeline nodes to stdout, showing each node's name, runtime, depth, noop status, and dependencies.

docs.name: eval
docs.brief: Evaluate a quoted expression or quosure
docs.full: Evaluates an Expression in the current environment, or a Quosure in its captured lexical environment. Returns plain values unchanged.

docs.name: read_arrow
docs.full: Reads a DataFrame from an Apache Arrow IPC (Feather v2) file.

params.name: path
params.description: The path to the Arrow file.

docs.name: explain
docs.full: Returns a dictionary describing the structure and content of a value.

return.description: A structured description of the value.

docs.name: var
docs.full: Compute sample variance.

docs.name: pipeline_to_frame
docs.full: Converts a Pipeline to a DataFrame where each row represents a node and each column represents a metadata field. This is a key inspection utility for understanding and debugging pipeline structure.  The columns returned are: - `name` — the node name (String) - `runtime` — one of "T", "R", "Python" (String) - `serializer` — e.g. "default", "pmml" (String) - `deserializer` — e.g. "default", "pmml" (String) - `noop` — whether the node is a no-op (Bool) - `deps` — names of nodes this node depends on (String, comma-separated) - `depth` — topological depth in the DAG (Int); roots are depth 0 - `command_type` — one of "command" or "script" (String)

return.description: A DataFrame with one row per node.

docs.name: to_integer
docs.full: Coerces a value to an integer robustly. Handles strings with spaces, percentages, commas, and recognizes 'TRUE'/'FALSE'.

docs.name: relocate
docs.brief: Move columns to a new position
docs.full: Reorders DataFrame columns by moving selected columns before or after another column.

docs.name: length
docs.full: Returns the number of elements in a collection (List, Vector, Dict). This function is NOT vectorized - it always returns the count of elements. For getting the number of characters in a string, use str_nchar() instead.

params.name: x
params.description: | Vector | Dict The collection to measure.

docs.name: prune
docs.full: Removes all leaf nodes — nodes that have no downstream dependents (nothing depends on them). This is useful for cleaning up intermediate pipelines after `filter_node` or `difference` operations.

return.description: A new pipeline with leaf nodes removed.

docs.name: make_period
docs.full: Builds a period from named year, month, day, hour, minute, and second components.

docs.name: t_make
docs.full: Builds a pipeline, defaulting to `src/pipeline.t`. This command can also pass arguments to the underlying Nix build, such as `--max-jobs`.

params.name: filename
params.description: The path to the pipeline file (defaults to "src/pipeline.t").

params.name: max_jobs
params.description: The maximum number of jobs for Nix to run in parallel.

params.name: max_cores
params.description: The maximum number of cores per job for Nix to use.

docs.name: deserialize
docs.full: Deserializes a value from a `.tobj` file.

docs.name: semi_join
docs.brief: Filter rows using matches in another table
docs.full: Keeps rows from the left DataFrame that have a matching key in the right DataFrame.

docs.name: fct_recode
docs.full: Recodes existing factor levels using named replacements.

docs.name: fct_lump_n
docs.brief: Keep the most frequent factor levels
docs.full: Collapses infrequent factor levels into an other bucket while keeping the most frequent levels.

docs.name: parse_file
docs.full: Scans a source file and extracts all T-Doc documentation blocks (lines starting with `--#`). Parses the tags (`@name`, `@param`, `@return`, etc.) into structured documentation entries.

params.name: filename
params.description: The path to the source file to parse.

return.description: A list of parsed documentation entries.

docs.name: is_numeric
docs.full: Predicate helper for numeric columns or numeric vectors.

docs.name: separate
docs.brief: Separate a character column into multiple columns
docs.full: Given either a regular expression or a fixed position, separate() splits a single character column into multiple new columns.

params.name: into
params.description: Names of the new columns to create.

params.name: sep
params.description: (Optional) Regular expression or position to separate at.

params.name: remove
params.description: (Optional) If true, remove the input column from the result.

docs.name: dispersion
docs.full: Returns the dispersion parameter of a Generalized Linear Model (GLM). For linear models (lm), use `sigma()` instead.
examples: model = glm(survived ~ age, data: df, family = "binomial")

docs.name: augment
docs.full: Appends model predictions, residuals, and potentially diagnostic metrics to a dataset.

return.description: The original DataFrame with appended `fitted`, `resid`, etc.

docs.name: is_error
docs.brief: Check if a value is an Error
docs.full: Returns true if the value is an Error object, false otherwise.

return.description: True if x is an Error.

docs.name: intent_get
docs.full: Retrieves a field from an Intent object.

docs.name: explain_json
docs.full: Returns a JSON string representation of the explain output.

docs.name: clean_names
docs.full: Normalizes a list of strings to be safe, consistent column names. Converts symbols (like €) to text, strips diacritics, lowers the case, replaces non-alphanumeric characters with underscores, and resolves duplicates.

docs.name: quantile
docs.full: Computes the quantile of a distribution at a specified probability.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: (Optional) Should missing values be removed? Default is false.

docs.name: build_pipeline
docs.full: Builds a pipeline to `pipeline.nix` and records node artifacts in a local registry.

return.description: The output path (Nix store path or local fallback directory).

docs.name: getwd
docs.full: Returns the absolute path of the current working directory.

docs.name: is_factor
docs.full: Predicate helper for factor columns or factor vectors.

docs.name: cummax
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative maximum of a vector.

docs.name: to_numeric
docs.full: Alias for `to_float`. Coerces a value to a numeric (float) robustly.

docs.name: clean_colnames
docs.full: Standardizes column names using a snake_case convention. Removes special characters and handles duplicates.

params.name: x
params.description: | List[String] The object with names to clean.

return.description: | List[String] The object with cleaned names.

docs.name: rename_node
docs.full: Renames a single node and rewires all dependency edges that referenced the old name to the new name. This is the canonical way to resolve name collisions before set operations.

params.name: old_name
params.description: The current name of the node.

return.description: A new pipeline with the node renamed.

docs.name: inspect_node
docs.full: Returns a dictionary with metadata about a computed node, including its name, runtime, artifact path, serializer, class, and dependencies.

params.name: node
params.description: A computed node value (e.g. from a built pipeline).

return.description: A dictionary with keys = name, runtime, path, serializer, class, dependencies.

docs.name: ceiling
docs.full: Return smallest integer greater than or equal to input.

docs.name: exit
docs.full: Terminates the current T process and accepts an optional numeric exit status.

docs.name: json_escape
docs.full: Escapes special characters for JSON compatibility.

docs.name: ordered
docs.full: Creates factor vectors marked as ordered for ordinal comparisons.

docs.name: swap
docs.full: Replaces a node's implementation with a new node value. The dependency edges of the replaced node are preserved — this operation only changes the node's command and metadata. Use `rewire` to change dependencies.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the node to replace.

return.description: A new pipeline with the node replaced.

examples: p |> swap("model_r", node(command = <{ lm(y ~ x, data) }>, runtime = R))

docs.name: help
docs.full: Prints the help documentation for the specified function, including signature, parameters, return value, and examples.

params.name: name
params.description: | Symbol The name of the function to document.

docs.name: filter
docs.full: Retains rows that satisfy the predicate function.

params.name: predicate
params.description: A function returning Bool for each row.

docs.name: assert
docs.full: Checks if a condition is true, raising an error if false.

docs.name: mutate
docs.full: Adds new columns or modifies existing ones.

docs.name: over
docs.full: Applies a function to the value focused by a lens and returns the updated structure.

docs.name: lm
docs.full: Fits a linear regression model using Ordinary Least Squares (OLS).

params.name: formula
params.description: The model formula (e.g., mpg ~ wt + hp).

return.description: A model object containing coefficients, residuals, and statistics.

docs.name: compare
docs.full: Align multiple model coefficient tables into a single wide DataFrame for comparison.

params.description: Models or a List of models to compare.

return.description: A wide DataFrame with aligned terms and suffixed columns.

examples: m2 = lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars)

docs.name: asinh
docs.full: Compute inverse hyperbolic sine.

docs.name: days_in_month
docs.brief: Get the number of days in a month
docs.full: Returns the number of days in the month described by a date, datetime, or explicit year/month pair.

docs.name: fct_c
docs.full: Combines multiple factor vectors while reconciling their levels.

docs.name: cbind
docs.full: HELPER: Combines two matrices by columns.

docs.name: glimpse
docs.full: Prints a summary of the DataFrame structure, including dimensions, column names, types, and first few values.

docs.name: %within%
docs.full: Returns true when a Date or Datetime value falls inside an interval.

docs.name: cv
docs.full: Compute sample sd divided by mean.

docs.name: cume_dist
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative distribution function (proportion of values <= current).

docs.name: shn
docs.full: A convenience wrapper around `node()` with `runtime = "sh"`. Use `shn()` inside a `pipeline { ... }` block to run POSIX shell commands or `.sh` scripts, and optionally set `shell = "bash"` when Bash parsing is required.

params.name: command
params.description: (Optional) The shell command or raw shell script body to execute. Mutually exclusive with `script`.

params.name: script
params.description: (Optional) Path to an external `.sh` file to execute as the node body. Mutually exclusive with `command`.

params.name: serializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom serializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: deserializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom deserializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: args
params.description: | List (Optional) Runtime arguments. Lists become positional CLI arguments for exec-style nodes.

params.name: shell
params.description: (Optional) Shell interpreter to invoke for shell-string mode or script-backed nodes. Default = "sh".

params.name: shell_args
params.description: (Optional) Additional arguments passed to the shell interpreter.

params.name: functions
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Additional files to include in the sandbox before execution.

params.name: include
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Additional files for the sandbox.

params.name: noop
params.description: (Optional) Whether to skip execution and generate a stub. Default = false.

return.description: The evaluated return value of the command.
| Null The file extension including the leading dot, or null if none.

docs.name: set
docs.full: Replaces the value focused by a lens with a new value.

docs.name: t_run
docs.full: Evaluates a T script file and imports its definitions into the current environment. Useful for interactive development to reload module files.

params.name: filename
params.description: The path to the T file to execute.

docs.name: rebuild_node
docs.full: Rebuilds a single node from the pipeline Nix expression and returns an updated ComputedNode with the new artifact path.

params.name: node
params.description: A computed node value to rebuild.

return.description: An updated ComputedNode pointing to the rebuilt artifact.

docs.name: parse_date
docs.full: Parses strings or string vectors into Date values using an explicit format string.

docs.name: node_lens
docs.full: Targets the cached result value of a specific node in a Pipeline.

return.description: A lens for the node's value.

docs.name: nest
docs.full: Packs selected columns into nested DataFrame values grouped by the remaining columns. Supports flexible column selection using symbols, strings, or selection helpers (like starts_with, ends_with).  If the DataFrame is already grouped (via group_by()) and no columns are specified, nest() will automatically nest all columns except the grouping keys.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Name for the new nested column, defaults to "data".
(Optional) Positional columns to nest if 'data' is not provided.

return.description: A new DataFrame with grouped keys and a nested list-column.

docs.name: asin
docs.full: Compute arcsine.

docs.name: pretty_print
docs.full: Prints a formatted representation of a value. DataFrames are printed as tables.

docs.name: exp
docs.full: Calculates e raised to the power of x.

return.description: | Vector | NDArray The result of e^x.

docs.name: crossing
docs.brief: Create a data frame from all combinations of inputs
docs.full: crossing() generates all unique combinations of its inputs. Unlike expand_grid(), it de-duplicates and sorts its inputs.

params.description: | List Named or unnamed inputs to combine.

return.description: A DataFrame with all unique combinations.

docs.name: tan
docs.full: Compute tangent (radians).

docs.name: force_tz
docs.brief: Retag a datetime with a timezone
docs.full: Reinterprets local clock components under a new timezone label.

docs.name: head
docs.full: Returns the first n items from a List, Vector, or DataFrame. For DataFrames, it returns the top n rows.

params.name: data
params.description: | List | Vector The collection to slice.

return.description: | List | Vector A subset of the input containing the first n items.

docs.name: get
docs.full: Retrieves a focused value from a data structure using a lens.

params.name: data
params.description: The data structure to focus on.

docs.name: mode
docs.full: Return most frequent value.

return.description: The most frequent value from the input (or NA if empty).

docs.name: rn
docs.full: A convenience wrapper around `node()` with `runtime = "R"`. Used directly within a `pipeline { ... }` block to execute R code.

params.name: command
params.description: (Optional) The expression to evaluate inside the R node (must be enclosed in `<{ ... }>` blocks). Mutually exclusive with `script`.

params.name: script
params.description: (Optional) Path to an external `.R` file to execute as the node body. Mutually exclusive with `command`. Sets the runtime to `R` automatically.

params.name: serializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom serializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: deserializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom deserializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: functions
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) R scripts to source before execution.

params.name: include
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Additional files for the sandbox.

params.name: noop
params.description: (Optional) Whether to skip execution and generate a stub. Default = false.

return.description: The evaluated return value of the command.

docs.name: pyn
docs.full: A convenience wrapper around `node()` with `runtime = "Python"`. Used directly within a `pipeline { ... }` block to execute Python code.

params.name: command
params.description: (Optional) The expression to evaluate inside the Python node (must be enclosed in `<{ ... }>` blocks). Mutually exclusive with `script`.

params.name: script
params.description: (Optional) Path to an external `.py` file to execute as the node body. Mutually exclusive with `command`. Sets the runtime to `Python` automatically.

params.name: serializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom serializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: deserializer
params.description: | Function (Optional) Custom deserializer strategy. Built-in values include "default", "arrow", and "pmml". Can be a string (e.g., "arrow") or an unquoted function name. Custom functions can also be used. Default = "default".

params.name: functions
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Python files to source before execution.

params.name: include
params.description: | List[String] (Optional) Additional files for the sandbox.

params.name: noop
params.description: (Optional) Whether to skip execution and generate a stub. Default = false.

return.description: The evaluated return value of the command.

docs.name: as_datetime
docs.full: Converts strings, dates, and related temporal values to Datetime values.

docs.name: t_write_json
docs.full: Serializes a T value to a JSON file. This is used as the universal baseline for object transport between runtimes in the sandbox interchange protocol.

docs.name: na_string
docs.full: Represents a missing string value.

docs.name: pipeline_depth
docs.full: Returns the maximum topological depth of any node in the pipeline. Root nodes have depth 0.

docs.name: pull
docs.full: Extracts a single column from a DataFrame as a Vector.

docs.name: file_exists
docs.full: Checks if a regular file exists at the given path. Returns false if the path is a directory or does not exist.

return.description: True if it's a regular file.

docs.name: min
docs.full: Returns the minimum value in a vector or list.

docs.name: t_read_json
docs.full: Deserializes a T value from a JSON file. Automatically handles type conversion for scalars, lists, and dictionaries.

docs.name: str_trim
docs.full: Removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string. Vectorized.

docs.name: contains
docs.full: Returns true if `sub` is present in `s`.

docs.name: str_join
docs.full: Concatenates items of a List or Vector into a single string, separated by `sep`.

docs.name: mad
docs.full: Compute scaled MAD: 1.4826 * median(|x - median(x)|).

docs.name: str_nchar
docs.full: Returns the number of characters in a string. Vectorized.

docs.name: downstream_of
docs.full: Returns a new pipeline containing the named node and all nodes that transitively depend on it (descendants in the DAG).

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the target node.

return.description: A new pipeline with only the node and its descendants.

docs.name: trunc
docs.full: Truncate fractional component toward zero.

docs.name: ceiling_date
docs.full: Rounds Date or Datetime values up to the requested unit boundary.

docs.name: group_by
docs.full: Groups a DataFrame by one or more columns for subsequent aggregation.

docs.name: str_detect
docs.full: Returns true when a regular expression matches a string.

docs.name: write_csv
docs.full: Writes a DataFrame to a CSV file.

docs.name: median
docs.full: Compute median of numeric values.

docs.name: is_na
docs.full: Checks if a value is NA (Not Available).

return.description: True if the value is NA.

docs.name: row_lens
docs.full: Targets a specific row in a DataFrame by its 0-based index.

return.description: A lens for the specified row.

docs.name: df_residual
docs.full: Returns the residual degrees of freedom of a model.

docs.name: pipeline_summary
docs.full: Returns a DataFrame with full metadata for every node in the pipeline. This is a convenience wrapper around `pipeline_to_frame`.

return.description: A DataFrame with one row per node and all metadata columns.

docs.name: ceil
docs.full: Alias for `ceiling`.

docs.name: cummean
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative mean of a vector.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: = false Remove NA values before computation.

docs.name: bind_cols
docs.full: Combines columns from multiple DataFrames side by side.

docs.name: subgraph
docs.full: Returns a new pipeline containing the named node, all of its ancestors, and all of its descendants — the full connected component reachable from the node in either direction.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the target node.

return.description: A new pipeline with the full connected component.

docs.name: where
docs.full: Selection helper that keeps columns for which a predicate function returns true.

params.description: One or more path segments to join.

docs.name: ungroup
docs.full: Removes the grouping structure from a DataFrame.

docs.name: package_info
docs.full: Returns metadata for a specific package, including its description and functions.

docs.name: list_files
docs.full: Returns a list of files and directories in the specified path. Supports an optional regex pattern for filtering.

params.name: path
params.description: [Optional] The directory to list. Defaults to ".".

docs.name: is_empty
docs.full: Returns true if the string has length 0.

docs.name: pipeline_copy
docs.brief: Copy Pipeline Node Artifacts to Local Directory
docs.full: Copies built artifacts from the Nix store to a local directory for easier inspection. By default copies all nodes from the latest build to `pipeline-output/`.

params.name: node
params.description: (Optional) The node name to copy. If null, copies all nodes.

docs.name: map
docs.brief: Map a function over a list
docs.full: Applies a function to each element of a list and returns a new list of results.

params.name: fn
params.description: The function to apply to each element.

docs.name: ndarray_data
docs.full: Returns the flattened data of an NDArray as a list of floats.

docs.name: make_date
docs.full: Builds a Date value from named year, month, and day components.

docs.name: make_datetime
docs.full: Builds a Datetime value from named date, time, and timezone components.

docs.name: row_number
docs.full: Returns the rank of the current row, breaking ties by index.

docs.name: winsorize
docs.full: Clamp tails to specified quantile limits.

params.name: limits
params.description: | Vector[Float] One-sided or (lo, hi) limits in [0, 0.5).

docs.name: cosh
docs.full: Compute hyperbolic cosine.

docs.name: body
docs.full: Returns the implementation body of a function. For T functions, it returns the body as an Expr object. For built-ins, it returns metadata about the OCaml implementation.

return.description: | String The function body or implementation info.

docs.name: env
docs.full: Retrieves the value of an environment variable.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the environment variable.

return.description: | Null The value of the variable, or null if not set.

docs.name: error_message
docs.full: Returns the human-readable message associated with an error.

docs.name: round
docs.full: Round numbers to a specified number of decimal digits.

docs.name: scaffold_project
docs.full: Generates the directory structure and boilerplate files for a new T data analysis project. Creates a `tproject.toml`, `flake.nix`, and sets up `data/`, `outputs/`, and `src/` directories.

docs.name: error_context
docs.full: Returns a dictionary containing contextual information about where and why the error occurred.

return.description: A dictionary of related context data.

docs.name: isoweek
docs.full: Returns the ISO week number for Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: enquo
docs.brief: Capture a function argument's expression (non-standard evaluation)
docs.full: Must be called inside a function body. Captures the unevaluated expression that the caller passed as the named parameter, returning it as an Expr. Accepts exactly one argument: a bare symbol naming one of the function's parameters.

params.name: param
params.description: The name of the parameter whose expression to capture.

docs.name: fct_rev
docs.full: Reverses the order of the levels in a factor vector.

docs.name: nrow
docs.full: Returns the number of rows in a DataFrame or the length of a vector.

docs.name: write_registry
docs.full: Writes a flat JSON object mapping node names to artifact paths.

docs.name: round_date
docs.brief: Round dates to the nearest unit
docs.full: Rounds Date or Datetime values to the nearest requested unit boundary.

docs.name: upstream_of
docs.full: Returns a new pipeline containing the named node and all of its transitive dependencies (ancestors in the DAG).

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the target node.

return.description: A new pipeline with only the node and its ancestors.

docs.name: union
docs.full: Returns a pipeline containing nodes from both inputs and errors on name collisions.

docs.name: atanh
docs.full: Compute inverse hyperbolic tangent.

docs.name: t_test
docs.full: Runs the test suite for the current package. Wraps the CLI `t test` command for use within the REPL.

return.description: Returns Null on success, or an Error if tests fail.

docs.name: ends_with
docs.brief: Check if string ends with suffix
docs.full: Returns true if `s` ends with the specified `suffix`.

return.description: True if s ends with suffix.

docs.name: unite
docs.brief: Combine multiple columns into one character column
docs.full: unite() is a convenience function that pastes together multiple columns into a single character column.

params.name: col
params.description: The name of the new column to create.

params.name: remove
params.description: (Optional) If true, remove the input columns from the result.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: (Optional) If true, missing values will be removed prior to uniting.

docs.name: filter_lens
docs.full: Targets elements in a List/Vector or rows in a DataFrame that satisfy a predicate.

return.description: A lens for elements matching the predicate.

docs.name: trace_nodes
docs.full: Prints a visual dependency tree of the pipeline nodes.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) A specific node's name to trace.

params.name: transitive
params.description: (Optional) If true, mark transitive dependencies with '*'.

return.description: Returns invisibly. Prints to the console.

docs.name: str_lines
docs.full: Splits on \n or \r\n. Strips trailing newline. Accepts ShellResult.

docs.name: max
docs.full: Returns the maximum value in a vector or list.

docs.name: hour
docs.full: Returns the hour component from Datetime values.

docs.name: fct_infreq
docs.full: Reorders factor levels so that more frequent levels appear first.

docs.name: difference
docs.full: Returns the nodes that appear in the first pipeline but not the second.

docs.name: drop_na
docs.full: drop_na() removes rows from a DataFrame where specified columns have missing values.

params.description: (Optional) Columns to check for missing values (use $col syntax).

return.description: The DataFrame with NA rows removed.

docs.name: signif
docs.full: Round to a fixed number of significant digits.

docs.name: ifelse
docs.full: Evaluates a condition and returns values from `true_val` or `false_val` depending on the condition. Supports missing value handling via the `missing` argument.

params.name: true_val
params.description: Expected return value when condition is true.

params.name: false_val
params.description: Expected return value when condition is false.

params.name: missing
params.description: (Optional) Value to return when condition is NA.

return.description: A vector of the resulting values.

docs.name: to_float
docs.full: Coerces a value to a float robustly. Handles strings with spaces, percentages, commas, and recognizes 'TRUE'/'FALSE'.

docs.name: packages
docs.full: Returns a list of all standard packages available in the environment.

docs.name: error_code
docs.full: Returns the error code as a string (e.g., "TypeError", "ValueError").

return.description: The error code as a string.

docs.name: na_int
docs.full: Represents a missing integer value.

docs.name: wday
docs.full: Returns weekday numbers, or weekday labels when requested, from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: levels
docs.full: Returns the level labels stored on a factor vector.

docs.name: matmul
docs.full: Performs matrix multiplication on two 2D NDArrays.

return.description: The product of the two matrices.

docs.name: iota
docs.full: Creates a vector of length n filled with 1.0. Use with `seq` or `ndarray` for more complex interactions.

docs.name: pf
docs.full: Returns cumulative probabilities from the F distribution.

docs.name: str_extract_all
docs.full: Returns every regular-expression match found in each string.

docs.name: pipeline_dot
docs.full: Returns a string containing a Graphviz DOT representation of the pipeline's dependency graph, suitable for visualization.

docs.name: lens
docs.full: Lenses provide a way to get and set values in nested structures. Supports column-based lenses for DataFrames and Dicts.

docs.name: log
docs.full: Calculates the natural logarithm (base e) of x.

params.name: x
params.description: | Vector | NDArray The input value (must be positive).

docs.name: error
docs.full: Raises a runtime error with a message and optional code.

docs.name: t_doc
docs.full: Documentation tools. Call with "parse" to extract docs from `src/`, or "generate" to output markdown files to `docs/reference/`.

docs.name: tail
docs.full: Returns the last n items from a List, Vector, or DataFrame. For DataFrames, it returns the bottom n rows.

params.name: data
params.description: | List | Vector The collection to slice.

return.description: | List | Vector A subset of the input containing the last n items.

docs.name: min_rank
docs.full: Assigns the minimum rank to ties, leaving gaps in ranks.

docs.name: day
docs.full: Returns the day-of-month component from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: cummin
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative minimum of a vector.

docs.name: tanh
docs.full: Compute hyperbolic tangent.

docs.name: na_bool
docs.full: Represents a missing boolean value.

return.description: The absolute path resolved against the current working directory.

docs.name: quo
docs.brief: Capture an expression with its lexical environment (quosure)
docs.full: Captures the provided expression as a **Quosure**: a pair of the expression AST and the current lexical environment. When later evaluated with `eval()`, the expression is evaluated in the captured environment, not the caller's. This matches the semantics of `rlang::quo()` in R.

params.name: x
params.description: The expression to capture as a quosure.

return.description: The captured expression with its environment.

docs.name: n_distinct
docs.full: Returns the number of distinct values in a vector or list. Inside `summarize()`, this acts as an aggregation expression.

docs.name: str_trunc
docs.full: Shortens strings to a maximum width and appends an ellipsis when needed.

docs.name: arrange_node
docs.full: Returns a new pipeline with nodes sorted by a metadata field. Execution order is always determined by the DAG — this affects only the order in which nodes appear when printing or serializing the pipeline.

params.name: field
params.description: The metadata field to sort by (e.g. `$depth`, `$name`).

return.description: A new pipeline with nodes reordered.

docs.name: sign
docs.full: Return -1, 0, or 1 depending on sign.

docs.name: args
docs.brief: Get function arguments and their types
docs.full: Returns a dictionary where keys are parameter names and values are their types. Supports both user-defined lambdas and builtin functions.
examples: f = \(x: Int, y = Float -> Int) x + y

docs.name: residuals
docs.full: Computes residuals for a model given a dataset.

docs.name: parallel
docs.full: Combines two pipelines that are intended to run independently. Errors immediately if any node name exists in both pipelines. Outputs are not automatically wired.

return.description: A merged pipeline with all nodes from both.

docs.name: idx_lens
docs.full: Targets an element in a List or Vector by its 0-based index.

return.description: A lens for the specified index.

docs.name: str_count
docs.full: Counts how many times a regular expression matches within each string.

docs.name: write_arrow
docs.full: Writes a DataFrame to an Apache Arrow IPC (Feather v2) file.

docs.name: cor
docs.full: Computes the Pearson correlation coefficient between two vectors.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: (Optional) Should missing values be removed? Default is false.

docs.name: sigma
docs.full: Returns the residual standard deviation (sigma) of a linear model. For GLMs, use `dispersion()` instead.

docs.name: count
docs.full: Counts rows in a DataFrame, optionally by selected columns or existing group keys.

docs.name: index_of
docs.full: Returns the index of the first occurrence of `sub` in `s`, or -1 if not found.

return.description: The index of the first occurrence.

docs.name: to_lower
docs.full: Converts all characters in the string to lowercase.

docs.name: sum
docs.full: Calculates the sum of values in a List or Vector.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: = false Remove NA values before summing.

docs.name: nobs
docs.full: Returns the number of observations used to fit a model.

docs.name: quos
docs.brief: Capture multiple expressions with their lexical environment (quosures)
docs.full: Captures one or more expressions as a List of Quosure values, each paired with the current lexical environment. Supports named arguments. Matches the semantics of `rlang::quos()` in R.

params.description: One or more expressions to capture as quosures.

docs.name: fit_stats
docs.full: Returns a tidy DataFrame of model-level statistics (e.g. R-squared, AIC, BIC). Supports single model objects or labeled collections of models for comparison.  When passed a list or dictionary of models, it stacks the results into a single DataFrame, automatically adding a 'model' column if labels are present.

params.name: x
params.description: | List[Model] | Dict[String, Model] The model(s) to inspect.

return.description: A tidy one-row-per-model summary of goodness-of-fit.

examples: m2 = lm(mpg ~ hp + wt, data = mtcars)

docs.name: pipeline_deps
docs.full: Returns a dictionary mapping node names to their dependencies.

docs.name: sin
docs.full: Compute sine (radians).

docs.name: transpose
docs.full: Returns the transpose of a 2D NDArray.

docs.name: fct_drop
docs.full: Removes levels that are not referenced by any value in the factor vector.

docs.name: str_format
docs.full: Substitutes {name} placeholders using values from a Dict or named List. Use {{ and }} to produce literal braces in the output.

params.name: values
params.description: | List The named values to substitute.

docs.name: intersect
docs.full: Returns the nodes from the first pipeline whose names also appear in the second.

docs.name: iqr
docs.full: Compute Q3 - Q1 using quantiles.

docs.name: casewhen
docs.full: Evaluates a series of `condition ~ value` formulas sequentially. Returns the value corresponding to the first true condition for each element.

params.description: One or more formulas of the form `condition ~ value`.

params.name: .default
params.description: (Optional) Value to return if no condition matches. Defaults to NA.

return.description: A vector of the matched values.

docs.name: vcov
docs.full: Returns the variance-covariance matrix of the model coefficients. For native T models, the full matrix is returned. For imported models, a diagonal matrix based on standard errors is returned as a fallback.

return.description: A square matrix representation with term names.

docs.name: source
docs.full: Returns the source code of a function as a string. For built-in functions, it attempts to read the underlying OCaml source file.

return.description: The source code of the function.

docs.name: read_log
docs.full: Fetches the Nix build log for a specific node from the last build attempt.

params.name: node_name
params.description: The name of the node to inspect.

docs.name: distinct
docs.full: Returns the distinct rows of a DataFrame, optionally using selected columns as uniqueness keys.

docs.name: dense_rank
docs.full: Assigns ranks to unique values, with no gaps.

docs.name: week
docs.full: Returns the week number for Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: minute
docs.full: Returns the minute component from Datetime values.

docs.name: run_doctor
docs.full: Validates the structure of a T package or project, checking for required files, directories, valid Nix configuration, and ensuring proper documentation setup.

return.description: Prints the validation results to the console.

docs.name: read_registry
docs.full: Parses a registry JSON file back into a list of name-path pairs.

docs.name: with_tz
docs.brief: Convert a datetime to a new timezone
docs.full: Retains the instant in time while changing the displayed timezone label.

docs.name: compose
docs.full: Combines two lenses into one, focusing on a value deep within a nested structure.

docs.name: huber_loss
docs.full: Compute Huber loss for residuals and positive delta.

docs.name: trimmed_mean
docs.full: Compute mean after trimming both tails by fraction.

docs.name: enquos
docs.full: Must be called inside a function body. Captures the unevaluated expressions passed through the variadic `...` parameter as a named List of Expr values. Call with `...` or with no arguments.

return.description: A list of captured expressions, preserving argument names.

docs.name: replace_first
docs.full: Replaces only the first occurrence of `old` with `new_` in `s`.

docs.name: abs
docs.full: Returns the absolute value of a number or vector/ndarray elements.

docs.name: second
docs.full: Returns the second component from Datetime values.

docs.name: cumany
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative logical OR of a vector.

docs.name: standardize
docs.full: Alias behavior for z-score standardization.

docs.name: modify
docs.full: Applies a sequence of lens-based transformations to the same data structure. Takes pairs of (lens, function).

params.description: Function Sequence of lens and transformation function pairs.

docs.name: now
docs.full: Returns the current datetime and accepts an optional timezone override.

docs.name: month
docs.full: Returns the month number, or month labels when requested, from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: scaffold_package
docs.full: Generates the directory structure and boilerplate files for a new T language package. Creates a `DESCRIPTION.toml`, `flake.nix`, `README.md`, and an initial test setup.

params.name: opts
params.description: The options provided via CLI (name, author, license, etc.).

docs.name: char_at
docs.full: Returns a single-character string at the specified index.

docs.name: yday
docs.full: Returns the day-of-year component from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: is_character
docs.full: Predicate helper for string columns or string vectors.

docs.name: as_date
docs.full: Converts strings, datetimes, and related temporal values to Date values.

docs.name: fill
docs.full: Fills missing values in selected columns using the next or previous entry.

params.name: .direction
params.description: (Optional) Direction in which to fill missing values.

docs.name: fct_collapse
docs.full: Merges several existing factor levels into new grouped levels.

docs.name: chain
docs.full: Connects two pipelines by merging them. The second pipeline can reference node names from the first pipeline as dependencies — these are automatically satisfied. Errors if there are name collisions (other than the intentional inter-pipeline wiring) or if no shared names exist between the two pipelines.

return.description: A merged pipeline with p2's nodes wired to p1's outputs.

docs.name: cos
docs.full: Compute cosine (radians).

docs.name: is_logical
docs.full: Predicate helper for boolean columns or boolean vectors.

docs.name: fct_relevel
docs.brief: Move selected levels to the front
docs.full: Explicitly reorders a factor by moving named levels ahead of the remaining levels.

docs.name: arrange
docs.full: Sorts a DataFrame by a column. Use "desc" for descending order.

docs.name: pipeline_cycles
docs.full: Returns a list of node names involved in dependency cycles. A well-formed pipeline should always return an empty list.

return.description: Names of nodes in cycles (empty if DAG is valid).

docs.name: select
docs.full: Selects specific columns from a DataFrame.

docs.name: to_array
docs.full: Converts numeric columns of a DataFrame to a matrix (NDArray).

params.name: cols
params.description: (Optional) Columns to include. Defaults to all numeric.

docs.name: cumall
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative logical AND of a vector.

docs.name: t_read_pmml
docs.full: Loads a PMML file from disk and returns its parsed model representation.

docs.name: predict
docs.full: Calculates predicted values for a linear model object. Supports models generated by lm() or imported via PMML and ONNX. Also supports PMML decision trees, random forests, and XGBoost/LightGBM.  For ONNX models, features are automatically resolved via model metadata or by matching numeric columns in order.

params.name: data
params.description: The new data used for prediction.

params.name: model
params.description: The linear / ensemble / ONNX model object.

docs.name: as_factor
docs.full: Alias for factor() that converts values to factor-encoded vectors.

docs.name: fct_expand
docs.full: Adds extra levels to a factor without changing existing assignments.

docs.name: range
docs.full: Return min and max as a length-2 vector.

docs.name: skewness
docs.full: Compute third standardized moment.

docs.name: parse_datetime
docs.full: Parses strings or string vectors into Datetime values using an explicit format string and optional timezone.

docs.name: tz
docs.full: Returns the timezone string attached to a Datetime value.

docs.name: dataframe
docs.full: Constructs a DataFrame from a list of dictionaries (rows).

docs.name: slice_max
docs.brief: Keep rows with the largest values
docs.full: Returns the rows with the highest values in an ordering column.

docs.name: everything
docs.full: Selection helper that returns every column name from a DataFrame.

docs.name: fct_lump_prop
docs.brief: Lump factor levels below a minimum proportion
docs.full: Collapses factor levels whose frequency falls below a proportion threshold.

docs.name: str_words
docs.full: Splits on whitespace, collapsing consecutive spaces. Accepts ShellResult. Note: words splits on spaces and tabs only, not newlines. For line-by-line processing use str_lines() first, then str_words() on each line.

docs.name: cov
docs.full: Compute sample covariance of two numeric vectors.

docs.name: sd
docs.full: Calculates the sample standard deviation of a numeric vector.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: (Optional) logical. Should missing values be removed? Default is false.

docs.name: lag
docs.full: Shifts a vector forward by n positions, filling with NA.

params.name: n
params.description: (Optional) Number of positions to shift. Default is 1.

docs.name: anti_join
docs.full: Keeps rows from the left DataFrame that do not have a matching key in the right DataFrame.

docs.name: str_split
docs.brief: Split a string on a delimiter
docs.full: Splits a string into a list of substrings on each occurrence of `sep`. If `sep` is empty, splits into individual characters. Works transparently on ShellResult values (splits stdout).

docs.name: kurtosis
docs.full: Compute fourth standardized moment minus 3.

docs.name: ndarray
docs.full: Creates a new NDArray from a list or vector of data, optionally specifying the shape. If shape is not provided, it is inferred from the nested structure of the input list.

params.name: data
params.description: | Vector The data to populate the array. Can be nested lists.

docs.name: semester
docs.full: Returns 1 for the first half of the year and 2 for the second half.

params.description: One or more expressions to capture.

docs.name: is_leap_year
docs.full: Returns true when the supplied year or date falls in a leap year.

docs.name: na
docs.full: Represents a missing value of generic type.

docs.name: last_index_of
docs.full: Returns the index of the last occurrence of `sub` in `s`, or -1 if not found.

return.description: The index of the last occurrence.

docs.name: run
docs.full: Executes a shell command and returns its stdout as a string. Raises a ShellError if the command fails (non-zero exit code).

docs.name: read_node
docs.full: Reads a node artifact from the latest (or specified) build log in `_pipeline/`. Use `which_log` to read from a specific historical build ("time travel").

params.name: which_log
params.description: (Optional) A regex pattern to match a specific build log filename.

docs.name: inner_join
docs.full: Joins two DataFrames and keeps only rows whose keys match in both inputs.

docs.name: quarter
docs.full: Returns the quarter number for Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: pivot_longer
docs.full: Lengthens data, increasing the number of rows and decreasing the number of columns.

params.description: The columns to pivot into longer format (use $col syntax).

params.name: names_to
params.description: (Optional) The name of the new column to hold the column names. Defaults to "name".

params.name: values_to
params.description: (Optional) The name of the new column to hold the values. Defaults to "value".

docs.name: coef
docs.full: Extracts the coefficient estimates from a model object, keyed by term name.

params.name: model
params.description: The model object (e.g., from lm() or imported).

docs.name: isoyear
docs.full: Returns the ISO week-based year for Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: read_parquet
docs.full: Reads a DataFrame from a Parquet file using the native parquet-glib reader.

params.name: path
params.description: Path or URL to the Parquet file.

docs.name: conf_int
docs.full: Computes confidence intervals for model coefficients based on the Student's t distribution.

params.name: model
params.description: The model object (e.g., from lm() or imported).

docs.name: kron
docs.full: Computes the Kronecker product of two matrices.

docs.name: fct_other
docs.full: Keeps selected factor levels and maps the rest to an other bucket.

docs.name: acos
docs.full: Compute arccosine.

docs.name: pm
docs.brief: Check whether a time is after noon
docs.full: Returns true for Datetime values whose hour is 12 or later.

docs.name: to_upper
docs.full: Converts all characters in the string to uppercase.

docs.name: fivenum
docs.full: Return min, Q1, median, Q3, max.

docs.name: pipeline_nodes
docs.full: Returns a list of node names in the pipeline.

docs.name: pt
docs.full: Returns cumulative probabilities from the Student t distribution.

docs.name: t_read_onnx
docs.full: Loads an ONNX model file from disk and returns a model dictionary. The resulting dictionary contains the model type identifier (^onnx), the file path, input/output names, and model-level metadata. This model object can be passed to `predict()` for native T-side inference.

params.name: path
params.description: The file path to the .onnx model.

docs.name: pipeline_leaves
docs.full: Returns the names of all leaf nodes — nodes that no other node depends on.

docs.name: atan
docs.full: Compute arctangent.

docs.name: apropos
docs.full: Searches all documented functions for names or descriptions matching the query.

docs.name: summarize
docs.full: Aggregates a DataFrame to a single row (or one row per group).

docs.name: lead
docs.full: Shifts a vector backward by n positions, filling with NA.

params.name: n
params.description: (Optional) Number of positions to shift. Default is 1.

docs.name: pnorm
docs.full: Returns cumulative probabilities from the standard normal distribution.

docs.name: pipeline_roots
docs.full: Returns the names of all root nodes — nodes that have no dependencies.

docs.name: inspect_pipeline
docs.full: Reads the latest (or specified) build log and returns a DataFrame showing the pipeline status.

params.name: which_log
params.description: (Optional) A regex pattern to match a specific build log filename.

return.description: A DataFrame with columns = derivation, build_success, path, output.

docs.name: am
docs.brief: Check whether a time is before noon
docs.full: Returns true for Date values and for Datetime values whose hour is earlier than 12.

docs.name: uncount
docs.full: Repeats each row according to a count column or weight expression.

docs.name: dir_exists
docs.full: Checks if a directory exists at the given path.

docs.name: complete
docs.full: Turns implicit missing values into explicit missing values. Supports nesting() to restrict combinations to those present in the data.

params.description: | Call Variable number of column names (use $col syntax) or nesting(...) calls.

params.name: fill
params.description: (Optional) A dictionary supplying a single value to use instead of NA for missing combinations.

params.name: explicit
params.description: (Optional) Should both implicit and explicit missing values be filled? (Default: true)

docs.name: expr
docs.full: Captures the provided expression as an Expr object without evaluating it. Useful for metaprogramming, quotation, and custom evaluation.

docs.name: pow
docs.full: Calculates base raised to the power of exponent.

return.description: | Vector | NDArray The result of base ^ exponent.

docs.name: slice
docs.full: Alias for `str_substring`. Returns the part of the string between `start` and `end` indices.

docs.name: matches
docs.full: Selection helper that returns columns whose names match a regular expression.

docs.name: pipeline_edges
docs.full: Returns a list of dependency pairs, each as a two-element list `[from, to]` representing an edge from a dependency to a dependent node.

docs.name: str_string
docs.full: Converts any value to its string representation.

docs.name: pipeline_validate
docs.full: Checks a pipeline for structural errors without throwing. Returns a list of error messages. An empty list means the pipeline is valid.  Checks performed: - No dependency cycles - All referenced dependencies exist as nodes in the pipeline

return.description: A list of validation error messages (empty = valid).

docs.name: interval
docs.full: Builds an interval from two Date or Datetime endpoints.

docs.name: serialize_to_file
docs.full: Serializes any T value to a file using OCaml's Marshal module.

docs.name: slice_min
docs.brief: Keep rows with the smallest values
docs.full: Returns the rows with the lowest values in an ordering column.

docs.name: mday
docs.full: Alias for day() that returns the day-of-month component from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: all_of
docs.brief: Select an explicit set of columns
docs.full: Selection helper that returns the supplied column names and errors if names are malformed.

docs.name: pivot_wider
docs.full: Widens data, increasing the number of columns and decreasing the number of rows.

params.name: names_from
params.description: The column whose values become output column names (use $col syntax).

params.name: values_from
params.description: The column whose values fill the new columns (use $col syntax).

docs.name: today
docs.full: Returns the current local date as a Date value.

docs.name: normalize
docs.full: Min-max normalize values to [0, 1].

docs.name: reshape
docs.full: Returns a new NDArray with the same data but different dimensions. The total number of elements must remain the same.

return.description: A new array with the specified shape.

docs.name: sqrt
docs.full: Calculates the square root of x.

params.name: x
params.description: | Vector | NDArray The input value (must be non-negative).

docs.name: factor
docs.full: Converts values to factor-encoded vectors with derived or explicit levels.

docs.name: diag
docs.full: If input is 1D, creates a diagonal matrix. If input is 2D, extracts the diagonal elements.

docs.name: cumsum
docs.full: Calculates the cumulative sum of a vector.

docs.name: str_sprintf
docs.full: Formats a string using C-style format specifiers. Supports %s (string), %d (integer), %f (float), and %% (literal %).

params.description: Values to substitute in the format string.

docs.name: any_of
docs.full: Selection helper that keeps the supplied column names when they are present.

docs.name: case_when
docs.full: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding value for the first true condition. Similar to SQL's CASE WHEN. Conditions are provided as formulas `condition ~ value`.

params.name: .default
params.description: (Optional) Default value if no condition is met.
Conditions and their corresponding return values.

return.description: A vector of the resulting values.

docs.name: env_var_lens
docs.full: Targets a specific environment variable for a node in a Pipeline.

params.name: var_name
params.description: The name of the environment variable.

return.description: A lens for the environment variable.

docs.name: print
docs.full: Prints one or more values to stdout, separated by spaces, followed by a newline. Strings are printed without quotes.

docs.name: n
docs.full: Returns the number of rows in the current aggregation context. Use this inside `summarize()` to count rows per group.

docs.name: fct_lump_min
docs.brief: Lump factor levels below a minimum count
docs.full: Collapses factor levels whose counts fall below a minimum threshold.

docs.name: build_pipeline_internal
docs.full: Calls `nix-build` on the generated `pipeline.nix` file. Extracts the store path of the result and saves a build log with an exact mapping of node names to artifact paths in the Nix store.

return.description: The output Nix store path or an error string.

docs.name: unnest
docs.full: Expands a nested list-column (produced by nest() or similar) back into its constituent rows and columns, effectively duplicating rows of the "parent" DataFrame for every row in the nested table.

params.name: df
params.description: The DataFrame containing a nested column.

params.name: cols
params.description: Selection column to unnest (positional or 'cols=' arg).

docs.name: ncol
docs.full: Returns the number of columns in a DataFrame.

docs.name: anova
docs.full: Compares nested models using F-tests (for linear models) or Chi-square tests (for GLMs).

params.description: One or more model objects to compare.

return.description: ANOVA table with statistics and p-values.

examples: m2 = lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars)

docs.name: poly
docs.full: Generates a basis of polynomial terms for a numeric vector.

params.name: raw
params.description: = false If true, return raw powers instead of orthogonal polynomials.

return.description: A named list of polynomial terms.

docs.name: mutate_node
docs.full: Modifies metadata fields on pipeline nodes. Supports a `where` named argument to scope changes to a subset of nodes. Without `where`, all nodes are affected.  Mutable metadata fields: `noop` (Bool), `serializer` (String), `deserializer` (String), `runtime` (String).  The `where` clause uses NSE (`$field`) just like `filter_node`.

params.name: where
params.description: (Optional) Predicate scoping which nodes are updated.

return.description: A new pipeline with updated node metadata.

docs.name: serialize
docs.full: Serializes a value to a `.tobj` file.

docs.name: percent_rank
docs.full: Calculates the proportional rank (0 to 1).

docs.name: full_join
docs.brief: Join all rows from both tables
docs.full: Joins two DataFrames and keeps rows appearing in either input.

docs.name: populate_pipeline
docs.full: Generates the `_pipeline/` directory with `pipeline.nix` and `dag.json`. Optionally builds the pipeline.

params.name: build
params.description: (Optional) Whether to trigger the Nix build immediately. Defaults to false.

return.description: A status message or the output path if build=true.

docs.name: acosh
docs.full: Compute inverse hyperbolic cosine.

docs.name: read_csv
docs.full: Reads a CSV file into a DataFrame.

params.name: path
params.description: Path or URL to the CSV file.

params.name: skip_lines
params.description: (Optional) Number of lines to skip at start.

docs.name: rename
docs.full: Renames selected DataFrame columns using named arguments.

docs.name: year
docs.full: Returns the calendar year from Date or Datetime values.

docs.name: starts_with
docs.brief: Check if string starts with prefix
docs.full: Returns true if `s` starts with the specified `prefix`.

return.description: True if s starts with prefix.

docs.name: str_substring
docs.full: Returns the part of the string between `start` and `end` indices.

docs.name: str_pad
docs.brief: Pad strings to a target width
docs.full: Pads strings on the left, right, or both sides until they reach a requested width.

docs.name: mean
docs.brief: Compute arithmetic mean of numeric values
docs.full: The mean is the sum of values divided by the count. This function handles NA values explicitly through the na_rm parameter.

params.name: x
params.description: | List[Float] Input numeric data. Must contain at least one value.

params.name: na_rm
params.description: = false Remove NA values before computation.

return.description: | NA The arithmetic mean, or NA if input contains NA and na_rm is false

docs.name: format_date
docs.full: Formats Date values with a user-supplied format string.

docs.name: type
docs.brief: Get the type name of a value
docs.full: Returns a string representation of the value's type (e.g., "Int", "String", "List").

docs.name: str_flatten
docs.full: Concatenates string collections into a single string with an optional separator.

docs.name: path_dirname
docs.brief: Get directory portion of a path

return.description: The directory portion of the path.

docs.name: str_replace
docs.full: Replaces all occurrences of `old` with `new_` in `s`.

docs.name: pipeline_run
docs.full: Re-executes a pipeline from start to finish.

docs.name: pipeline_assert
docs.full: Validates the pipeline and returns it unchanged if valid. Throws the first validation error found if the pipeline is invalid.

docs.name: format_datetime
docs.full: Formats Datetime values with a user-supplied format string.

docs.name: sinh
docs.full: Compute hyperbolic sine.

docs.name: pipeline_node
docs.full: Retrieves the value of a specific node in the pipeline.

docs.name: write_text
docs.full: Writes a string to a file at the specified path.

docs.name: seq
docs.full: Creates a list of integers from start to end, optionally with a step size.

params.name: end
params.description: (Optional) Ending value. Defaults to start if by is provided.

docs.name: cat
docs.full: Prints one or more values to stdout using a custom separator. Unlike print(), it does not add a trailing newline and supports custom separators. Strings are printed raw (with escape sequences like \n interpreted).

docs.name: floor
docs.full: Return greatest integer less than or equal to input.

docs.name: intent_fields
docs.full: Returns all fields of an Intent object as a dictionary.

docs.name: path_basename
docs.brief: Get filename component of a path

return.description: The final component of the path.

docs.name: pchisq
docs.full: Returns cumulative probabilities from the chi-squared distribution.

docs.name: score
docs.full: Calculates various performance metrics (RMSE, MAE, R-squared, etc.) for a model on a dataset.

docs.name: add_diagnostics
docs.full: augments the data with model diagnostic columns (residuals, fitted values, etc.).

return.description: The data with added diagnostic columns.

docs.name: list_logs
docs.full: Lists all available build logs in the `_pipeline/` directory.

return.description: A DataFrame of build log files with their modification times and sizes.

docs.name: update_flake_lock
docs.full: Regenerates the `flake.nix` file from the current TOML configuration and runs `nix flake update` to lock dependencies to their latest versions within the specified tags.

docs.name: left_join
docs.brief: Join rows from the left table
docs.full: Joins two DataFrames and keeps every row from the left-hand side.

docs.name: summary
docs.full: Returns a tidy DataFrame of regression coefficients and statistics.

return.description: Tidy summary dictionary with `_tidy_df` and metadata.

docs.name: cut
docs.full: Splits a numeric vector into intervals.

params.name: breaks
params.description: | Vector[Number] Number of bins or specific cut points.

docs.name: rewire
docs.full: Reroutes a node's declared dependencies. The `replace` argument is a named list (or Dict) mapping old dependency names to new ones. Only the named node's dependency list is updated.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the node whose deps should change.

params.name: replace
params.description: A named list mapping old dep names to new ones.

return.description: A new pipeline with updated dependency edges.

docs.name: scale
docs.full: Standardize to z-scores using sample standard deviation.

docs.name: filter_node
docs.full: Returns a new pipeline containing only the nodes for which the predicate returns `true`. Uses NSE (`$field`) to refer to node metadata fields.  No DAG validity check is performed. If a retained node depends on a node that was removed, that inconsistency surfaces only at `build_pipeline` or `pipeline_run`.  Supported metadata fields: `$name`, `$runtime`, `$serializer`, `$deserializer`, `$noop`, `$depth`, `$command_type`.

params.name: predicate
params.description: A predicate function returning Bool for each node.

return.description: A new pipeline with only the matching nodes.

docs.name: inv
docs.full: Computes the multiplicative inverse of a square matrix.

docs.name: json_unescape
docs.full: Decodes escaped characters from a JSON string.
